Introduction to Swisher County

Arrows in Swisher County

Arrow #30: Tulia

More Details for Arrow #30

Coordinates: 34.5362977, -101.7718295

Installed: April 20, 2012

Dedicated: July 21, 2012

Marker Text:

Quanah Parker's peyote blanket and other Indian artifacts are part of Swisher County's Museum collection

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #31: Tulia

More Details for Arrow #31

Coordinates: 34.5248182, -101.7630874

Installed: April 20, 2012

Dedicated: July 21, 2012

Marker Text:

Comanche and other Indian tribes camped and hunted along the Tule Creek

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #32: Tulia

More Details for Arrow #32

Coordinates: 34.5637509, -101.7227328

Installed: April 20, 2012

Dedicated: July 21, 2012

Marker Text:

Comanche and other Indian tribes camped near natural springs to the east and nearby playa lakes

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #35: Tule Canyon

More Details for Arrow #35

Coordinates: 34.4769535, -101.5353528

Installed: June 15, 2012

Dedicated: July 21, 2012

Marker Text:

Quanah Parker never forgot that near this site Sept 29, 1874 the U.S. Army shot 1,048 Indian horses

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Map of Arrows in Swisher County

Texas Plains Trail Region Map

Click a county to view arrows

Welcome to the Quanah Parker Trail!

Explore the historical arrow markers commemorating Quanah Parker and the Comanche people across the Texas Plains Trail Region. Use the map on the homepage or navigate via the menu to learn more about this effort.
